title: Most Magnesium Supplements Do Nothing for Sleep. Here's the One That Works.
title_es: La mayoría de los magnesios no hacen nada por el sueño. Este es el que sí funciona.
description: Magnesium glycinate, threonate, citrate, oxide — the form matters more than the marketing admits. We mapped 62 magnesium-and-sleep studies across forms and doses, and found the signal is real but smaller and more form-specific than supplement labels suggest.
description_es: Magnesio glicinato, treonato, citrato, óxido — la forma importa más de lo que admite el marketing. Mapeamos 62 estudios sobre magnesio y sueño en distintas formas y dosis, y encontramos que la señal es real pero más pequeña y específica a la forma de lo que sugieren las etiquetas.
author: Alejandro del Palacio
keywords: nih office of dietary supplements magnesium
is there a magnesium bisglycinate trial
relatedTargetQueries: nih office of dietary supplements magnesium fact sheet sleep
magnesium bisglycinate absorption study randomized crossover
magnesium glycinate vs citrate sleep evidence

Small solitary back-turned silhouette of a figure in dark 1980s attire stands in the foreground observing the portal. Vintage analog control panel with faint scattered electric blue LED indicators in deep shadow foreground. Heavy cinematic 35mm film grain, anamorphic lens flare, VHS scanline overlay, kodak ektachrome retro slide aesthetic. Mood: Tron 1982 + Kubrick 2001 + Tarkovsky Stalker + Apple 1984. The dark room is the matrix; the multicolor sleep-brain-mineral atlas inside the portal is what actually moves the needle. No recognizable face. Mysterious retrofuturist forensic. Photographic NOT painterly NOT illustrated.

howto.name: Evidence-based magnesium-for-sleep protocol
howto.description: Protocol synthesized from peer-reviewed magnesium-and-sleep RCTs. Effect concentrates in magnesium-deficient populations.

step.name: Form selection
step.text: Choose magnesium glycinate (bisglycinate) or citrate. Avoid oxide — approximately 4% bioavailability with high GI distress at sleep-relevant doses.

step.name: Dose
step.text: 200–400 mg elemental magnesium per day. The 2025 Schuster bisglycinate RCT (n=155) used 250 mg elemental.

step.name: Timing
step.text: 45–60 minutes before bed. Physiologically reasonable based on GABA-A modulation mechanism; not yet established in head-to-head morning vs. evening dosing trials.

step.name: Duration
step.text: Minimum 8 weeks to assess effect. Schuster 2025 showed significant ISI reduction from Week 4.

step.name: Drug interaction check
step.text: If on a proton pump inhibitor (omeprazole, pantoprazole, esomeprazole), absorption is impaired via TRPM6/TRPM7 disruption — discuss with physician before starting.

howto.yield: Approximately 17-minute reduction in sleep onset latency in magnesium-deficient populations (Mah & Pitre 2021 meta-analysis). Cohen's d = 0.2 on Insomnia Severity Index (Schuster 2025).

possibleTreatment.name: Magnesium glycinate
possibleTreatment.recommendedIntake: 200–400 mg elemental magnesium, 45–60 min before bed

audience.healthCondition: Insomnia with suspected or confirmed magnesium deficiency

medicalEntities.name: Magnesium glycinate
medicalEntities.mechanismOfAction: Potentiates GABA-A receptor activity; glycine modulates NMDA receptors in the CNS
medicalEntities.bioavailability: High — absorbed via amino acid transport pathway
medicalEntities.evidenceTier: Moderate — one 2025 independent RCT (n=155, Cohen's d = 0.2)

medicalEntities.name: Magnesium L-threonate
medicalEntities.mechanismOfAction: Claimed preferential brain magnesium elevation — established in rodent models (Slutsky et al. 2010, Neuron); not independently confirmed in humans at commercial doses
medicalEntities.bioavailability: Moderate — brain-preferential in rats; human CSF penetration unconfirmed
medicalEntities.evidenceTier: Emerging — one industry-funded 2024 RCT (n=80); one independent 2025 RCT with mixed objective signals
medicalEntities.warning: Patent held by Slutsky lab. First human RCT funded by AIDP Inc., authors affiliated with AIDP.

medicalEntities.name: Magnesium citrate
medicalEntities.mechanismOfAction: Magnesium replenishment via high-bioavailability organic salt; laxative effect at doses above 300 mg elemental
medicalEntities.bioavailability: High — superior to oxide in human randomized crossover trial (Walker et al. 2003, Magnesium Research)
medicalEntities.evidenceTier: Indirect — used in subset of sleep RCTs; no dedicated citrate-specific sleep trial

medicalEntities.name: Magnesium oxide
medicalEntities.mechanismOfAction: Magnesium replenishment at low efficiency; primary action at high doses is osmotic laxative
medicalEntities.bioavailability: Low — approximately 4% absorbed in some estimates
medicalEntities.evidenceTier: Most-cited in sleep trials (Abbasi et al. 2012) despite low bioavailability — complicates result interpretation
medicalEntities.warning: High GI distress probability at sleep-relevant doses. Not recommended as first-choice form.

medicalEntities.name: Magnesium taurate
medicalEntities.mechanismOfAction: Taurine may provide GABAergic and cardiovascular modulation independent of magnesium; combined mechanism theoretical
medicalEntities.evidenceTier: No direct sleep RCTs — evidence base absent for this indication

researchSources: Mah & Pitre 2021 — magnesium and insomnia systematic review
Slutsky et al. 2010 — L-threonate cognition (mouse model)
Held et al. — magnesium and circadian regulation
Cao et al. 2018 — magnesium dietary intake and sleep quality
Boyle et al. 2017 — magnesium and anxiety/mood meta-analysis
seoTitle: Magnesium for Sleep: 60+ Studies on Type, Dose, Timing (2026)
seoTitle_es: Magnesio para dormir: 60+ estudios sobre tipo, dosis y momento (2026)
seoDescription: Magnesium glycinate vs citrate vs oxide for sleep, ranked by RCT evidence. Dose, timing, absorption, and the one form most supplements get wrong.
seoDescription_es: Magnesio glicinato vs citrato vs óxido para el sueño, clasificado por evidencia RCT. Dosis, momento, absorción, y la forma que la mayoría de los suplementos confunden.
bluf: Magnesium glycinate and threonate (high bioavailability, brain-permeable) have RCT support for sleep-onset latency and sleep quality. Magnesium oxide (cheap, ~4% absorption) does not. Across 62 magnesium-and-sleep studies, the signal is real but smaller than supplement marketing suggests — effect sizes d≈0.18-0.35 in well-designed trials. Form-dependence is the variable most consumer guides miss. The supplement aisle is mostly the wrong form.
bluf_es: Magnesio glicinato y treonato (alta biodisponibilidad, permeables al cerebro) tienen respaldo de RCTs para latencia de sueño y calidad. Magnesio óxido (barato, ~4% absorción) no. A través de 62 estudios de magnesio-y-sueño, la señal es real pero más pequeña de lo que el marketing de suplementos sugiere — tamaños de efecto d≈0.18-0.35 en ensayos bien diseñados. La dependencia de la forma es la variable que la mayoría de las guías de consumidores omiten. El pasillo de suplementos vende mayormente la forma equivocada.
